veal-treats-teleculinaria.name: Veal Treats
instructions: Season the meat and shrimps with garlic, previously peeled and crushed, salt and pepper.
Grate the medallions as well as the bacon slices, the bat and the shrimps, removing them from the heat as they get cooked.
Cut the bat into thick slices, arrange on a platter with the medallions and shrimps and serve with chips.

vegan-mushroom-thanksgiving-stuffing.name: Vegan Mushroom Thanksgiving Stuffing
instructions: Spread cubed bread on a baking sheet. Cover with a paper towel and let sit at room temperature until slightly stale, at least 8 hours to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 149°C (150 degrees C).
Toss stale bread cubes with olive oil and sprinkle with thyme, rosemary, oregano, parsley, garlic salt, and white pepper. Spread evenly on the baking pan.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes. Stir and continue baking until crispy, about 20 minutes more, checking frequently. Remove from the oven and set homemade croutons aside.
Increase oven temperature to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a large baking pan.

Heat margarine in a large pan over medium heat. Add celery, onion, mushrooms, and garlic. Cook and stir until soft, about 5 minutes. Add broth and white pepper and simmer for 3 minutes more. Add homemade croutons and stir, transferring mixture to a large bowl if necessary. Pour stuffing mixture into the prepared baking pan.
Bake in the hot oven, uncovered, for about 40 minutes.

vegetarian-piri-piri-chorizo-bake.name: Vegetarian Piri Piri Chorizo Bake

ingredients.prep: or use 2 (9-oz.) bags microwavable basmati rice, to serve

instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C
First, cook the sweet potatoes. Peel the sweet potatoes and cut them into 3/4-inch cubes.

Cut the bell peppers in half, cut out the stems and seeds, and cut the flesh into 3/4-inch cubes, then put them in the roasting pan. Use the back of a knife to lightly crush the 3 garlic cloves and add them to the pan. Drizzle with a little olive oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Add the sweet potatoes to the peppers and put the pan in the oven for 10–15 minutes, until the peppers have small black patches on the skins.
Meanwhile, cut up store-bought sausages into bite-sized pieces and cook following the instructions on the package.

To make the piri piri sauce, peel and roughly chop the onion and garlic. Cut the bell pepper in half and cut out the stem and seeds. Rip the stems from the chiles, cut them in half lengthwise, and remove the seeds if you prefer a milder sauce. Put the onion, garlic, pepper, and chiles in the blender with the paprika, oregano, red wine vinegar, and basil. Grate in the zest of the lemon, then cut it in half and squeeze in the juice, catching any seeds. Add a drop of water and blend to a smooth paste. Taste and adjust the seasoning, if necessary.

Remove the roasting pan from the oven. Transfer the sausages and piri piri sauce to the pan and mix everything together. Add the cherry tomatoes and put the pan back in the oven for 15 minutes, until the potatoes and peppers are cooked and the sauce is piping hot.
Heat the rice or cook it following the instructions on the package.
Pluck the leaves from the cilantro and discard the stems. Coarsely chop the leaves and sprinkle them over the vegetables.
Serve with rice.

very-berry-rhubarb-bars.name: Very Berry Rhubarb Bars
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 13x9-inch baking dish.
Make crust: Mix together flour, brown sugar, and cinnamon in a large bowl until well combined. Cut butter into flour mixture until crumbs form; press into the bottom of the prepared baking dish.
Bake crust in the preheated oven until golden brown, about 15 minutes.
Make filling: Blend sugar, eggs, flour, and salt in a blender until smooth; pour into a large bowl. Fold in rhubarb, strawberries, and blueberries until incorporated, then spread onto baked crust.
Make topping: Mix together flour, brown sugar, oats, melted butter, and cinnamon in a medium bowl until well combined; sprinkle over fruit mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until lightly browned on top and the filling has set, 45 to 50 minutes.

vichyssoise-in-thermomix-pequerecetas.name: Vichyssoise In Thermomix

ingredients.item: 3 leeks, only the white part washed
2 patatas medianas, peladas y cortadas en trozos
Fur to add an aromatic touch

instructions: Peel his leek washing him under the cold water jet. Eliminates the root and green part of the stem. Also removes the first layer of the leek by making a longitudinal cut from top to bottom. He cuts and cuts his onion and cuts it into pieces and puts his leg cut into his pits and his onion and program for 4 seconds at Speed 5.
Agrega el aceite y la mantequilla, y programa durante 12 minutos a temperatura Varoma, velocidad 1.
Add 200 g of water and program for 1 minute at 5-10 progressive speed to ensure that the leek and onion are well shredded.
Incorporates crisped potato, salt, pepper and remaining water. Programme for 20 minutes at 100ºC, Speed 1.
Espera unos minutos hasta que la temperatura baje un poco y programa durante 1 minuto a velocidad 7.
It incorporates nutmeg and cream. Programme for 8 minutes at 90 ° C, 3. If more clear consistency is preferred, water can be added as a matter of taste.

victoria-cake-argiro.name: Victoria Cake
instructions: Butter 2 baking pans of 20 cm.
Preheat the oven to 160o C, in the air. We'll fry on the middle grill.
For Victoria sponge sandwich cake all materials must be at room temperature and butter must be very soft. I used soft butter and the result was very fluffy puffy cake.
We'll use four big eggs or 250 g eggs. The flour for the cake must be sifted.
In fact, we make 2 cake inflatable and fluffy, and we turn them into a cake sandwich, putting a layer of red jam and whipped cream in between.
It's delicious and impressive as a cake made of fluffy cake.

village-goat-teleculinaria.name: Village Goat
instructions: Fix the goat, cut it into pieces if necessary, and season it with finely chopped garlic teeth, white wine, pepper paste, half olive oil, lemon juice with lemon after squeezed cut into pieces, parsley branch, blond leaves, salt and pepper. Rub well and let marinate overnight in the fridge.

On the day, start the oven at 180 degrees. Lay the goat on the tray, water it with marinade and the rest of the olive oil, add the margarine to pieces on top and bring it to the oven for 1 hour. Turn around and water with your own sauce once in a while.
Peel and wash the chips, add them to the tray, wrap them in the sauce and let them bake for about 30 minutes or until the potatoes are soft. Remove from the oven and serve decorated to taste.
